projectDescription: Open-source ESP32 firmware that bridges modern Bluetooth controllers (PS3/4/5, Xbox, Switch, Wii, and generic HID) to retro game consoles and computers including NES, SNES, N64, GameCube, PlayStation, Saturn, Dreamcast, and many more.
keyFeatures: Multiplayer support with up to 4+ simultaneous Bluetooth controllers via a single adapter
Support for 20+ retro consoles: NES, SNES, N64, GameCube, PlayStation 1/2, Saturn, Dreamcast, 3DO, Jaguar, PC-Engine/TG16, and more
Compatible with PS3, PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X|S, Nintendo Switch, Wii, and generic HID Bluetooth (BR/EDR & LE) devices
Fully configurable button mapping, axis calibration, and dead zone settings via web interface
Multitap support for consoles with multi-port adapters
Macro button combos for system commands (reset, power off, factory reset, Bluetooth inquiry)
Game ID detection for automatic per-game configuration profiles
Virtual memory card emulation for PlayStation and other consoles
Open source hardware and firmware under CERN-OHL-P-2.0 and Apache-2.0 licenses
Built on ESP32 with ESP-IDF framework
publicApiSurface: adapter_bridge() — translates Bluetooth HID input to wired console output
adapter_bridge_fb() — forwards console feedback (rumble, LEDs) to Bluetooth device
config_init() / config_update() — load and persist adapter configuration
bt_host_init() — initialize the custom Bluetooth host stack
sys_mgr_cmd() — issue system management commands (reset, power, factory reset)
mc_read() / mc_write() — virtual memory card read/write interface
gid_update() — update game ID from console feedback data
Wired system IDs: PARALLEL_1P, PARALLEL_2P, NES, PCE, GENESIS, SNES, CDI, REAL_3DO, JAGUAR, PSX, SATURN, PCFX, JVS, N64, DC, PS2, GC, WII_EXT, VBOY
Bluetooth device types: BT_HID_GENERIC, BT_PS3, BT_WII, BT_PS, BT_SW, BT_SW2
